resources.string: The active network is explicitly marked as VPN by Android.
Lockdown mode appears active — no non-VPN path is validated.
This is the strongest signal in the app: Android reports TRANSPORT_VPN on the network currently in use.
Android sees a VPN network in the system, but not on the current active network.
This often matches bypass or split-tunnel behavior: a VPN exists, but current traffic may not be fully routed through it.
Android APIs still expose VPN-like indicators even though active TRANSPORT_VPN is absent.
This is weaker than a direct VPN transport flag, but interface, DNS, or capability signals still suggest VPN-related state in the visible network stack.
No primary Android VPN signal was found, but tunnel-like interfaces were still discovered.
This usually means only low-level interface heuristics fired. It is useful as an additional hint, but weaker than official Android VPN signals.
No active VPN network signal was found, but known VPN-related apps are installed on the device.
Installed VPN apps do not prove that a VPN is currently active, but they are still a relevant contextual signal.
The app did not find any high-level or low-level VPN indicators.
Neither official Android network APIs nor interface enumeration produced a VPN-related signal.
TRANSPORT_VPN is present on the active network.
A VPN-related transport exists system-wide, but it is not the current active path.
No active TRANSPORT_VPN, but Android APIs still expose VPN-related information.
No VPN transport was reported by Android.
Android did not report VPN transport on the active path.
The interface name itself looks like a tunnel device and matches the active VPN state.
The interface name looks tunnel-like and is consistent with a VPN being present somewhere in the system.
The interface name looks tunnel-like, but Android does not currently mark the active path as VPN.
The interface name does not look like a typical VPN or tunnel interface.
Android did not expose an interface name for this network.
Android returned transport info alongside an active VPN transport.
Transport info is present and aligns with a VPN existing somewhere in the network stack.
Transport info is present, but without a direct active VPN transport flag.
No VPN-related transport info was exposed here.
Native enumeration found interfaces whose names or properties look tunnel-like.
Native enumeration did not find any tunnel-like interfaces.
Java network enumeration found interface names that look like VPN or tunnel interfaces.
Java network enumeration did not find any tunnel-like interface names.
No interfaces were returned by the native detector.
Check errors (package manager returned unexpected error):
This app holds Android VPN permission (anomalous for a detector).
VPN present and no validated non-VPN path exists. Lockdown mode is likely active.
%1$d user profiles detected. VPN apps in other profiles are not visible to this detector.
